Mission Point Health Campus of Jackson

Overview

Address
703 Robinson Road, Jackson, MI 49203
Phone
5177875140
Certified beds
50
Avg daily residents
44 (87% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1993-07-01
Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.88 4.52 3.32 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.96 0.79 0.82 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.13 1.00 0.97 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.79 2.72 1.53 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 2.09 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.07 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.44
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.42
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.94
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
62.3%
annual
RN turnover
57.1%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.60
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.16
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-12-12 9 8 1 60 1 60
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-01-17 26 11 15 148 1 148
Total weighted health score
82.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (46)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

45 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2026-01-29 2026-02-13
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-12-12 2025-01-17
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-17
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-17
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2024-12-12 2025-01-17
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2024-12-12 2025-01-17
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-12-12 2025-01-17
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2024-12-12 2025-01-17
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. E 2024-12-12 2025-01-17
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-09-27 2024-10-18
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2024-09-05 2024-10-11
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2024-09-05 2024-10-11
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-09-05 2024-10-11
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2024-03-21 2024-02-13
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-03-21 2024-04-12
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-12
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-12
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-12
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-12
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-12
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-12
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-12
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2024-01-17 2024-02-12
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-12
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2024-01-17 2024-02-12
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. F 2024-01-17 2024-02-12
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2023-12-20 2024-01-12
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2023-11-21 2023-12-03
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2023-10-26 2023-11-13
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. D 2023-03-30 2023-05-05
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2023-03-30 2023-05-05
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. D 2023-03-30 2023-05-05
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2023-03-30 2023-05-05
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-03-30 2023-05-05
0839 Employ staff that are licensed, certified, or registered in accordance with state laws. E 2023-03-30 2023-05-05
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2022-10-11 2022-11-08
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2022-10-11 2022-11-08
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2022-10-11 2022-11-08
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2022-10-11 2022-11-08
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2022-10-11 2022-11-08
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2022-10-11 2022-11-08
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2022-10-11 2022-11-08
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2022-10-11 2022-11-08
0825 Provide or get specialized rehabilitative services as required for a resident. D 2022-10-11 2022-11-08
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. F 2022-10-11 2022-11-08
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. F 2022-10-11 2022-11-08

Methodology & sources
